title: f30: podman fails to start my toolbox containers
body:
I had these toolbox containers for a while and they were working fine. Recently, I had to switch from rawhide to F30, for some unrelated issue. And now, the toolboxes fail to start.

This is with podman 1.4 in F30:

```
$ podman --log-level debug start fedora-toolbox-mclasen-30
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000                
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
DEBU[0000] overlay: mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs 
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=false, usingMetacopy=false 
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] overlay: mount_data=lowerdir=/var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/3Q23VHSYPTRCAOCQQNJ2LWMAIO:/var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/CJLT4B66MFIDFGPA4VMHFWLLPP:/var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/2BLCYUCG6LQGD2RFK3FXPXBFJA,upperdir=/var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/0cb0c108d8d87ca235ceea1feb61878bbab45817e008e6e30c93c22f0e0a4827/diff,workdir=/var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/0cb0c108d8d87ca235ceea1feb61878bbab45817e008e6e30c93c22f0e0a4827/work,context="system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c373,c1001" 
DEBU[0000] mounted container "3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a" at "/var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/0cb0c108d8d87ca235ceea1feb61878bbab45817e008e6e30c93c22f0e0a4827/merged" 
DEBU[0000] Created root filesystem for container 3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a at /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/0cb0c108d8d87ca235ceea1feb61878bbab45817e008e6e30c93c22f0e0a4827/merged 
DEBU[0000] /etc/system-fips does not exist on host, not mounting FIPS mode secret 
WARN[0000] User mount overriding libpod mount at "/dev/shm" 
DEBU[0000] set root propagation to "rslave"             
DEBU[0000] Created OCI spec for container 3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a at /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a/userdata/config.json 
DEBU[0000] /usr/libexec/podman/conmon messages will be logged to syslog 
DEBU[0000] running conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon    args=[-c 3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a -u 3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a -n fedora-toolbox-mclasen-30 -r /usr/bin/runc -b /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a/userdata -p /run/user/1000/overlay-containers/3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a/userdata/pidfile --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --conmon-pidfile /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000 --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg error --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg cgroupfs --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg runc --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a --socket-dir-path /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket -l k8s-file:/var/home/mclasen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a/userdata/ctr.log --log-level debug --syslog]
WARN[0000] Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/libpod_parent: permission denied 
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied

WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
DEBU[0000] Cleaning up container 3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a 
DEBU[0000] Network is already cleaned up, skipping...   
DEBU[0000] unmounted container "3500dffd6f91012b2e510a24ed6b7bd9035f1d8587ad7777cae886837b41f89a" 
ERRO[0000] unable to start container "fedora-toolbox-mclasen-30": error reading container (probably exited) json message: EOF 
```

comments.body:
Debarshi points out that 

```
rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/*/userdata/*
```

works around this breakage.
can you spot any other error message in the journal?  Seem like it is failing in runc

I see:
```
Jun 21 11:04:26 localhost.localdomain conmon[6312]: conmon 46aebaf6ade512d54033 <ndebug>: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
Jun 21 11:04:26 localhost.localdomain kernel: SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
Jun 21 11:04:26 localhost.localdomain conmon[6313]: conmon 46aebaf6ade512d54033 <ndebug>: container PID: 6324
Jun 21 11:04:26 localhost.localdomain conmon[6313]: conmon 46aebaf6ade512d54033 <ninfo>: attach sock path: /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a/attach
Jun 21 11:04:26 localhost.localdomain conmon[6313]: conmon 46aebaf6ade512d54033 <ninfo>: addr{sun_family=AF_UNIX, sun_path=/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a/attach}
Jun 21 11:04:26 localhost.localdomain conmon[6313]: conmon 46aebaf6ade512d54033 <error>: Failed to bind attach socket: /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a/attach: Address already in use
Jun 21 11:04:26 localhost.localdomain podman[6313]: 2019-06-21 11:04:26.816768156 +0800 +08 m=+0.096128079 container cleanup 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a (image=registry.fedoraproject.org/f30/fedora-toolbox:30, name=fedora-toolbox-30)
```

Edit: updating to podman-1.4.2-1.fc30 (from updates-testing) didn't help.
Maybe running `podman stop` before rebooting, avoids this problem.
@juhp could you share your `~/.config/containers/libpod.conf` and  `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` files?
`/run/user/1000/libpod` should be empty after a reboot.  Is it on a tmpfs?

```
$ cat ~/.config/containers/libpod.conf
volume_path = "/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes"
image_default_transport = "docker://"
runtime_path = ["/usr/bin/runc", "/usr/sbin/runc", "/usr/local/bin/runc", "/usr/local/sbin/runc", "/sbin/runc", "/bin/runc", "/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc"]
conmon_path = ["/usr/libexec/podman/conmon", "/usr/libexec/crio/conmon", "/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon", "/usr/local/libexec/crio/conmon", "/usr/bin/conmon", "/usr/sbin/conmon", "/usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon"]
conmon_env_vars = ["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
init_path = "/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit"
static_dir = "/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod"
tmp_dir = "/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp"
max_log_size = -1
no_pivot_root = false
cni_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"
cni_plugin_dir = ["/usr/libexec/cni", "/usr/lib/cni", "/usr/local/lib/cni", "/opt/cni/bin"]
infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
infra_command = "/pause"
enable_port_reservation = true
label = true
$ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
  driver = "overlay"
  runroot = "/run/user/1000"
  graphroot = "/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage"
  [storage.options]
    mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```

Also
```
$ df /run/user/1000/
Filesystem     1K-blocks  Used Available Use% Mounted on
tmpfs            1606492   192   1606300   1% /run/user/1000
```

I was not able to reproduce the exact issue, but I can force `start` to fail by creating the `attach` file before starting the container.

I've opened https://github.com/containers/conmon/pull/46 so that conmon will unlink the existing file before trying to create a socket there.  It solves the case where I manually create it.  Would it be possible for you to try it out?

Once you build the conmon binary, you can try something `podman --conmon /path/to/the/new/executable start CONTAINER`

@giuseppe not sure how you are testing - I imagine it is easy to reproduce on Silverblue at least with Toolbox.

I tested the patch but it doesn't completely fix the problem:

```
$ podman --log-level debug start fedora-toolbox-30
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
WARN[0000] The configuration is using `runtime_path`, which is deprecated and will be removed in future.  Please use `runtimes` and `runtime` 
WARN[0000] If you are using both `runtime_path` and `runtime`, the configuration from `runtime_path` is used 
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000                
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
DEBU[0000] overlay: mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs 
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=false, usingMetacopy=false 
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] overlay: mount_data=lowerdir=/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/MUWGZWQJABJ7XJGJQ5QAXHHYYG:/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/ULGN7VVCYUYYXTUU7R5G3QQW4X,upperdir=/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/d1f10b0d0c2dae1dce544f12c7fc34ec767eebb8212af8cbc589abcef185f6a9/diff,workdir=/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/d1f10b0d0c2dae1dce544f12c7fc34ec767eebb8212af8cbc589abcef185f6a9/work,context="system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c841,c886" 
DEBU[0000] mounted container "46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a" at "/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/d1f10b0d0c2dae1dce544f12c7fc34ec767eebb8212af8cbc589abcef185f6a9/merged" 
DEBU[0000] Created root filesystem for container 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a at /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/d1f10b0d0c2dae1dce544f12c7fc34ec767eebb8212af8cbc589abcef185f6a9/merged

DEBU[0000] /usr/libexec/podman/conmon messages will be logged to syslog 
DEBU[0000] running conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon    args=[-c 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a -u 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a -n fedora-toolbox-30 -r /usr/bin/runc -b /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a/userdata -p /run/user/1000/overlay-containers/46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a/userdata/pidfile --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --conmon-pidfile /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000 --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg error --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg cgroupfs --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg runc --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a --socket-dir-path /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket -l k8s-file:/var/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a/userdata/ctr.log --log-level debug --syslog]
WARN[0000] Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/libpod_parent: permission denied 
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied

WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
DEBU[0000] Cleaning up container 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a 
DEBU[0000] Network is already cleaned up, skipping...   
DEBU[0000] unmounted container "46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a" 
ERRO[0000] unable to start container "fedora-toolbox-30": error reading container (probably exited) json message: EOF 
```

Edit: I cannot reproduce on a Fedora WS 30 vm though. So maybe it is specific to Silverblue.

These are the files I see in my `userdata/`:
```
$ ls -R /home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/b78e30369f5884a6cee4c9c8abac9279908a2af7b024f61881cc505e987da69f/userdata/
/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/b78e30369f5884a6cee4c9c8abac9279908a2af7b024f61881cc505e987da69f/userdata/:
artifacts  attach  config.json  conmon.pid  ctl  ctr.log

/home/petersen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/b78e30369f5884a6cee4c9c8abac9279908a2af7b024f61881cc505e987da69f/userdata/artifacts:
create-config
```
The main difference compared to on F30 Workstations seems to be the presence of `conmon.pid`.

So, after a debugging session with @giuseppe and @mheon, it turned out that for me, the problem was that I had multiple containers with a lock ID of 0, which occurred in 1.1/1.2 for various reasons, and with 1.3 and later, causes the 'refresh' operation after a reboot to fail, and the conmon temporary files not to be cleaned up.

Workarond is to run `podman system renumber` and reboot.

I see interesting, thanks, Owen!

I had indicative trouble with running `podman system renumber`.
Before rebooting I got one error, which I should have noted down, and after reboot I got:

```
$ podman system renumber
ERRO[0000] Error refreshing container 05c3f05f4a5c151f6b00167fba76eeb2106cc32568bec80051e2fdd6456ac751: error acquiring lock for container 05c3f05f4a5c151f6b00167fba76eeb2106cc32568bec80051e2fdd6456ac751: file exists 
ERRO[0000] Error refreshing container 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a: error acquiring lock for container 46aebaf6ade512d540334427598998bcef3c0dc47c075751427f915be035220a: file exists 
ERRO[0000] Error refreshing container b78e30369f5884a6cee4c9c8abac9279908a2af7b024f61881cc505e987da69f: error acquiring lock for container b78e30369f5884a6cee4c9c8abac9279908a2af7b024f61881cc505e987da69f: file exists 
ERRO[0000] Error refreshing container fbaa95c5af2959ab738a8d78206358ff65abfc372b963d259af8b573c7899d01: error acquiring lock for container fbaa95c5af2959ab738a8d78206358ff65abfc372b963d259af8b573c7899d01: file exists 
```

But after (re)moving `~/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/containers.lock` those errors went away and I am now able to reboot to reworking toolboxes without having to remember to `podman stop -a`.
